svg.text: TTLSim 16V8 burns — GAL16V8 test harness on Arduino Mega 2560
Identical physical wiring to the Mini Blinky harness — no rewiring, only the sketch changes. D22 / D24 stay Mega INPUTS for all three chips.
chip-pin labels read: simple / registered / complex

g.text: C1: 100 nF ceramic across pins 20–10, close to the socket

svg.text: D22 / D24 never change direction for these chips: Mega INPUT (idle) on all three — series resistors stay fitted and are harmless.
Physical view — bottom-edge header, left → right (unchanged from Mini Blinky harness)
board edge below • inner-row pins D24 / D22 sit directly opposite D25 / D23 at the right-hand end
Per-burn pin assignments (pin 20 = 5 V, pin 10 = GND on all three)

g.text: ¹ D22 / D24 (GAL pins 12/13) are unused by all three burns. Leave the series 470 Ω – 1 kΩ resistors fitted; the Mega keeps these pins as inputs the whole time.
² Hi-Z check for y2 (complex burn): set D27 to INPUT_PULLUP, apply a = b (driven y2 would be 0) with ena = 0 — reading 1 proves the pin floated (tri-state works).
³ /OE check (registered burn): drive D23 high with INPUT_PULLUP on D25/D27/D29 — all read 1 regardless of counter state, proving the registered outputs went Hi-Z.
Clocking (registered burn): pulse D53 low→high→low per step; read q2 q1 q0 while the clock is low. Sequence 000→001→…→111→000; rst = 1 forces 000 on the next edge.
